title: Create resolve-pr-comments skill
description: Add a dedicated skill that addresses PR review comments using a structured per-comment subagent workflow. Triggers: user wants to resolve open PR review comments (Copilot, human, or both). Workflow: clarify ambiguity only when judgment cannot, dispatch one subagent per fix, use ralf-it for significant work, run completion gate (code-review + code-simplifier + verify-checklist), commit back to PR, reply to each comment with what was fixed/skipped + reason, GH-resolve conversations only for FIXED comments. Lives in src/user/.agents/skills/resolve-pr-comments/SKILL.md (shared content, copied to all tools).

title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

Look for:
- Duplication (three similar lines is a smell; two is acceptable)
- Names that require a comment to understand
- Logic that could be expressed more simply
- Dead code

Re-run tests after every change. If tests break, you changed behavior.
Undo the change and try again.

This step is NOT about adding features or improving performance.
It is about making the code readable by the next person (who is you,
in six months, having forgotten everything).
close_reason: Refactor phase: 3 prose-clarity fixes to root README — simplified dense prerequisites callout, de-bloated Tool-specific extensions bullet into sub-bullets, fixed alphabetical order in skills table. Subfolder READMEs, CONTRIBUTING, LICENSE, and AGENTS.md drift-fix reviewed — no changes needed.

title: implement-feature
description: TDD feature implementation: brainstorm → worktree → red/green/refactor → completion gate → PR → close

title: Brainstorm: requirements for Documentation pass for public repo readiness
description:
Invoke the brainstorming skill BEFORE any design, tests, or code.

Skill: superpowers:brainstorming

Questions to resolve:
- What problem does this actually solve?
- Who uses it and how?
- What does "done" look like? (acceptance criteria)
- What are the edge cases and error paths?
- Any architectural implications or constraints?
- Is there a simpler approach that solves the same problem?

Output: written acceptance criteria and a clear implementation plan.

Write criteria back to the bead so it persists across sessions:
  bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance="<one line per criterion>"

If anything is ambiguous or contradictory, stop and ask the user
before proceeding to the next step.
close_reason: Brainstorming completed upstream via brainstorm-bead formula. Spec, requirements, and acceptance criteria live in parent bead agents-config-mxe (notes + acceptance fields), already ralf-reviewed.

title: Set up isolated git worktree
description:
Invoke the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated branch.

Skill: superpowers:using-git-worktrees

- Branch from main (never from another feature branch)
- Verify you are IN the worktree directory before writing any code
- Confirm no leftover state from other work

Then claim the bead:
  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

If the bead has a parent epic, mark that in_progress too:
  bd show {{bead-id}}   # check for parent
  bd update <parent-id> --status in_progress

title: Implement Documentation pass for public repo readiness (green phase)
description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.
close_reason: Implemented docs pass: LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, 4 subfolder READMEs (src/user/.agents, .claude, .codex, .gemini), drift fixes in README.md/AGENTS.md (tree diagram adds src/plugins/; manual install adds rules/ copy; Agents section lists names; Skills table verified 1:1; Commands section removes non-shipping /implement-bead; Prerequisites/Note/Customizing-Templates corrected re: location of orchestration/delegation/completion-gate/delivery/beads content; AGENTS.md rules/ list corrected). Removed obsolete src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md stub. 5 commits on branch. Session Completion/Beads dedup in root AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md not performed: those blocks are inside auto-generated BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 markers and are out of scope per spec.

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:
- Tests must be written against the acceptance criteria from the brainstorm step
- Cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand
- Do NOT write test-only methods in production code

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.
close_reason: N/A for docs-only change: no executable code paths to unit test. Acceptance criteria in parent bead are already formulated as falsifiable grep/filesystem checks, which will be executed at the Completion-gate verify-with-evidence step as the equivalent of test verification.

title: Create pull request
description:
Invoke the finishing-a-development-branch skill.

Skill: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

- Push the branch to origin
- Create a PR with a clear summary linked to {{bead-id}}
- PR title should be ≤70 characters
- PR body should include: what changed, why, and how to test it

Do NOT merge. Do NOT squash. Await review in the next step.

title: Completion gate: code review
description:
Dispatch code-reviewer subagent against ALL changed files.

Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer
Scope: every file changed since branch creation, reviewed against:
  - The implementation plan from the brainstorm step
  - SOLID and DRY principles
  - Security by design (OWASP top 10, input validation, no injection paths)
  - Architectural consistency with the rest of the codebase
  - The standards in INSTRUCTIONS.md

Address ALL findings before proceeding. If a finding is deferred,
document the rationale explicitly.

For high-stakes changes (security-sensitive, architecture shifts):
  /codex:adversarial-review --wait --model gpt-5.4
close_reason: Code review complete: 6 files reviewed, findings addressed: (1) plugin contents drift fixed (README+AGENTS line ~44 'commands' -> 'skills'); (2) AC #9 non-actionable due to plugin markers, discovered-work bead agents-config-5e3 filed to address upstream; (3) trailing newline nit was already correct. Final diff has 7 focused commits.

title: Await PR review and triage feedback
description:
Invoke the wait-for-pr-comments skill.

Skill: superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments

- Wait for Copilot review to complete (or time out)
- Check BOTH top-level PR comments AND inline review comments
  (inline comments are easy to miss — always verify both)
- Auto-fix unambiguous feedback
- Report findings and triage results to the user

Do NOT merge or clean up the worktree until review is complete
or has explicitly timed out.
close_reason: Copilot review: 4 inline suggestions addressed (installer behavior accuracy), pushed as single commit. No top-level review decision (copilot-pull-request-reviewer uses COMMENTED state).

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.
close_reason: Verify-with-evidence gate: 13/14 ACs satisfied with direct evidence. AC9 deferred — blocked by plugin-managed BEADS INTEGRATION markers; discovered-work bead agents-config-5e3 filed to address upstream. Tests/build/lint/typecheck N/A for docs-only change.

title: Completion gate: simplify
description:
Dispatch code-simplifier subagent against changed code.

Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier

This step exists because the implementing agent always misses
at least one DRY violation or clarity issue. It is not optional.

Address ALL findings. Re-run tests after any changes made here.
close_reason: Final simplify pass: no changes needed. The two-word fix in 8e33344 (commands → skills) is factually accurate — verified against src/plugins/beads/ structure (.agents/skills/, .claude/rules/, .beads/formulas/). Both README.md L44 and AGENTS.md L42 are internally consistent with surrounding context. Docs-only lines, no clarity or redundancy issues introduced.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. If all siblings in a parent epic are now closed, close the parent:
     bd show {{bead-id}}    # check for parent
     bd list --parent=<parent-id>  # check siblings

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation:
     bd create "Found: ..." -t bug -p 1
     bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Housekeeping: 2 insights recorded via bd remember (plugin markers, install.sh auto-detect); discovered-work bead agents-config-5e3 filed earlier; parent bead agents-config-mxe left in_progress pending explicit merge authorization (per delivery rules, implement-feature formula ends at await-review, merge requires separate user authorization).

title: Documentation pass for public repo readiness
acceptance_criteria:
LICENSE file at repo root contains MIT license text and names Scott Hamilton as copyright holder with year 2026.
CONTRIBUTING.md at repo root states repo is a personal-config project, asks contributors to open an issue first, references file format conventions in AGENTS.md.
README.md Customizing Templates section no longer instructs readers to edit CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md for delegation, completion-gate, delivery, or git-commits content; those references point to rules/.
README.md Manual install block tells readers to copy src/user/.claude/rules/ into ~/.claude/rules/ (currently missing).
README.md repository tree diagram includes src/plugins/ alongside the existing subfolders.
README.md Skills table is bidirectionally aligned with src/user/.agents/skills/ by directory name: every skill directory has a table row, every row corresponds to a real directory.
README.md Agents section lists which specific agents ship (by name) or explicitly points to the directory listing.
Root AGENTS.md Repository Structure matches actual tree; BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 marker block is left intact (auto-generated — do not edit).
Root AGENTS.md non-generated duplication with CLAUDE.md (Session Completion, Beads Issue Tracker Quick Reference) is reduced by pointing one doc at the other rather than repeating verbatim.
src/user/.agents/README.md exists and explains shared content purpose, install target, and audience.
src/user/.claude/README.md exists and explains Claude-specific content; the 4-line src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md stub is deleted in the same PR that adds the README.
src/user/.codex/README.md exists and explains Codex-specific content.
src/user/.gemini/README.md exists and explains Gemini-specific content.
No edited or added doc contains a reference to a file path, section heading, or skill that does not exist in the repo after the change.
notes:
# Documentation pass for public repo readiness

## Summary
Prepare the agents-config repo for public consumption. Scope: polish existing top-level docs, correct known drift, and fill gaps a first-time visitor expects (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, per-subfolder READMEs). No GitHub-specific scaffolding, no prose-level rewrites.

## Background / motivation
Repo is going public. Current state: root README.md is detailed but has drift from the rules/ reorganization; root AGENTS.md has duplicated session-completion blocks; tool-specific subfolders under src/user/ have no or near-empty READMEs; no LICENSE file exists despite README declaring MIT; no CONTRIBUTING.md. A stranger landing on the repo needs accurate install guidance and a clear picture of what each folder contains.

Primary reader: fork-and-install users, not contributors. Light contributor notes only.

## Requirements
- Add a root LICENSE file matching the MIT license already declared in README.md. Copyright holder: "Scott Hamilton". Year: 2026.
- Add a short CONTRIBUTING.md acknowledging the repo is a personal config and asking contributors to open an issue first.
- Add a README.md to each tool-specific subfolder: src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, src/user/.codex/, src/user/.gemini/. Each explains purpose, contents, install target, and intended audience.
- Correct drift in existing root README.md and root AGENTS.md: any stale references to file paths, section names, or structure that have since moved (notably content relocated from CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md to rules/).
- Verify README.md Skills table and Agents section against the actual contents of src/user/.agents/skills/ and src/user/.agents/agents/.
- Delete the 4-line stub at src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md in the same PR that adds src/user/.claude/README.md (README added first, stub removed after).

## Out of scope
- .github/ scaffolding (issue templates, PR templates, workflows)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- Restructuring or rewriting the root README for style
- Changes to docs/plans/ or docs/specs/ content
- Changes to src/plugins/AGENTS.md (already in good shape) — but root README tree SHOULD mention src/plugins/
- Any non-doc changes (no code edits, no install.sh changes)
- Any edits inside the root AGENTS.md BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 marker block — auto-generated by the beads plugin, regenerated on bd runs. Only the non-generated Session Completion and Beads Quick Reference blocks that appear outside or alongside the markers should be deduplicated.

## Design notes

### Drift detection (README.md / AGENTS.md)

Concrete checks — after edits, these greps should return nothing (or only current-truth results):

- No instruction to "copy CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template" without also mentioning rules/.
- No claim that `<orchestration>`, `<delegation>`, `<completion-gate>`, `<delivery>`, or `<beads>` sections live in CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md. They live in INSTRUCTIONS.md (orchestration) and rules/ (the rest).
- No reference to a skill name that doesn't correspond to a directory in src/user/.agents/skills/.
- No reference to an agent name that doesn't correspond to a file in src/user/.agents/agents/.

### Skills/Agents verification

Bidirectional by directory name:
- Every directory in src/user/.agents/skills/ has a row in the README Skills table.
- Every row in the README Skills table corresponds to a real directory.
- Agents section either lists the shipping agent names or points to the directory; do NOT leave it as vague prose.

### Beads integration markers

Root AGENTS.md contains `<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 ... -->` and `<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->` markers around a plugin-managed block. This block is regenerated by the beads plugin on install and must not be edited by hand. Any deduplication work on AGENTS.md must target content OUTSIDE these markers.

### Subfolder README style

- Short (roughly 20-50 lines each). Short enough that a first-time visitor can scan.
- Link up to the root README for install flow; do not duplicate install instructions.
- Answer three questions per folder: what lives here, how does it get installed, who is it for.

### LICENSE

Canonical MIT license text. Format:
```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2026 Scott Hamilton

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, ...
```

### CONTRIBUTING.md

Example draft:
> This is currently a personal configuration repository. If you find it useful and want to contribute agents, skills, or commands, please open an issue to discuss before sending a PR. See AGENTS.md for file format conventions (agent frontmatter, skill SKILL.md layout, command markdown).

### Delivery

- Single branch, single PR.
- Docs-only. No code changes, no install.sh changes, no settings changes.
- Drift fixes are surgical — edit only stale claims, not prose style.

## Open questions
- None blocking. Minor copy-edit decisions during implementation are fine to decide inline.
close_reason: Merged via PR #7 (squash commit d194c06). All ACs satisfied except AC9 which was deferred and tracked as agents-config-5e3 (plugin-managed duplication requires upstream fix).

title: Beads plugin duplicates Session Completion block in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md
description: The beads plugin's BEADS INTEGRATION marker block renders the same 'Session Completion' and 'Beads Issue Tracker' content into both root AGENTS.md and root CLAUDE.md. This is plugin-managed (inside BEGIN/END BEADS INTEGRATION markers) so manual deduplication is impossible. Options: (a) file upstream issue against the beads plugin to support a 'pointer-only' profile for the secondary file, or (b) add a non-plugin pointer pattern in our templates and stop using the minimal profile for CLAUDE.md. Discovered during agents-config-mxe public-repo docs pass.

value: scripts/install.sh auto-detect logic: always includes 'claude' (lines 237-239), adds 'codex'/'gemini' only if ~/.codex/ or ~/.gemini/ exists, OR if --tools= is passed explicitly. Do NOT describe installer as 'detects CLI presence' — it doesn't; it checks for config directories.

beads plugin auto-generates Session Completion and Beads Quick Reference blocks inside <!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 ... --> markers in BOTH root AGENTS.md and root CLAUDE.md. These blocks are identical by design (rendered from the same template), regenerated on bd runs, and must NOT be manually edited. Cross-file deduplication is impossible without upstream plugin changes. Discovered during agents-config-mxe docs pass; follow-up tracked in agents-config-5e3.
